item.title: CAPSULE x hololive production x “Cheer UP” Event Comes to Los Angeles | COVER Corp. - カバー株式会社
hololive SUPER EXPO 2025 Report [Part 2]: A Festival of VTuber Culture Enjoyed With All Five Senses
item.author: cover_yamaguchi
item.content:encoded: Treasuring Unity and Memories: A Diversity of Booths Creating a True Arcade ExperienceThe Libestal Castle and Sanctuary designs drew visitors to the exhibit, giving them a sense of what it felt like to be a part of the story. Story details were displayed, allowing avid fans to read more about the settings, characters, and dwell in the actual stories that happened in the first Chapter, which aired back in September 2024. Standees featured the hololive English talents proudly donning their Libestal Cloaks.Q. What did you put extra detail into for the exhibits so visitors could enjoy the most out of hololive English?As the EXPO is held in Japan, some people will not be familiar with the event culture here. However, we wanted to create an experience where visitors could feel like they were in that world rather than just introduce the content to those who were entirely new. We made sure that this was an exhibit where fans could feel the nostalgia and bring back fond memories when they visited for themselves.Q. ENigmatic Recollection Chapter 2 has been released this month. We’re already at the edge of our seats to see future developments of this project!I believe that the biggest appeal of this project was the unexpected developments (sometimes emotional, and other times humorous), combined with an original story, lore, and tales that arise from the talents’ freedom to express themselves.At this year’s EXPO, we felt real passion from the fans, which made me even more conscious that we must work together to continue hyping up the community.Moving forward, we would like to create even more amazing content where fans are not just bystanders in these stories, but actively involved as a part of the world.The hololive OFFICIAL CARD GAME (hereinafter holoCards) booth, holoCard in Wonderland, was a whimsical journey, a seamless blend of hololive and Alice in Wonderland. While the giant card display, dramatically suspended from the ceiling, was a major eyecatcher amongst the booths, the card game class, and giveaways of a special cheer card and sample deck were also charming souvenirs that fans could take home.Q. Please tell us about the concept of the booth and what went into its making.Firstly, to adopt the concept of Alice in Wonderland, we needed to put great detail into staging: things like making small things look gigantic, and building a space where one could feel a sense of wonder at first sight. Secondly, we had the challenge of expanding a domain unique to holoCards. Currently, the cards are centered around the talents’ activities and personalities, but in the future, we want to take on a new challenge of bringing out new aspects and stories of talents by incorporating distinguishing themes and concepts.Q. What was an instance in EXPO that left a deep impression on you?I once again felt the warmth of the hololive community. There were a lot of posts on social media of fans coming to EXPO and interacting with each other, some of whom discovered and became a fan of hololive because of holoCards through social media. Seeing more experienced fans showing first-timers the ropes and introducing them to the community was really impactful.One of our goals as producers was to introduce people to hololive through holoCards, come to like the content, and to grow the community together as fans. Seeing this becoming a reality before my eyes gave me a sense of pride and reward for the commitment I had put into this project.Mochipoyo-chan was created in 2023 as an original character project, with the concept of being cute and lovable. In this booth, we created a fully immersive photo space with themed designs on all the walls and floors, making it feel as if visitors were transported to the world of Mochipoyo-chan. Fans were scrambling to the interactive exhibits that could be touched and felt, such as the gigantic Mochipoyo-chan plush and the Mochipoyo Wall, in which lots of planning went into creating their textures. Many fans were posting on social media, using the hashtag #もちぽよちゃん (#mochipoyochan), pictures of their oshi’s plushie at the plushiephotospotwe had set up with a Mochipoyo-chan plush.Q. What was the response like from fans?We had all kinds of fans coming and enjoying the soft sensation of the gigantic Mochipoyo-chan plushie and Mochipoyo Wall. This booth was originally targeted at women, so we were worried about how men, who make up the majority of fans at EXPO, would feel about it. Still, we were happy that we had a constant line of people over the two days, and fans of all demographics could enjoy the exhibit.It seemed that the Mochipoyo Wall was particularly impactful, as many visitors briefly came to lay their hands on it as they passed by, making it a great meet-up spot for fans. I was glad to see people responding positively to the many autographs from hololive members who were not included in the Mochipoyo-chan designs around the exhibit. We had an astounding 2,383 responses to the questionnaire set in three locations of the booth, and we have high hopes to expand the variety of content, such as merchandise and anime.Q. Where did the idea for the plushie photospot, which seemed popular among fans, come from?We recalled seeing many fans taking pictures of their oshi’s plushes with the gigantic Mochipoyo at the previous EXPO, so we asked our events team at the end of last year if we could create a photo spot with a special stand just for fans put their plushes on to take pictures, knowing it was too much to ask with the time available. However, despite the short notice, we had a spot for fans to take pictures in such a popular and adorable space. We were extremely grateful that we were able to pull it off thanks to them!“All about your Oshi. One app.” The concept of holoplus ARCADE, the booth for the official hololive production app holoplus, was a cool-looking arcade with an abundance of vibrant lights and neon signs, with an animated logo as the centerpiece. The community notebook placed in the booth was scattered with heartwarming messages for the app, oshis, and Harusaki Nodoka, the publicity manager of holoplus.Q. What was your focus when designing this booth?As well as building an atmosphere that everyone can enjoy within such a limited space, we aimed to make it one that promotes communication and where memories can be left. We placed a “communication notebook” that is a familiar sight at arcades in the booth as well, through which not only did the management team express to users their gratitude and excitement for the future, but we were so happy to receive so many messages of support for the management team as well!Q. What was the response like from fans?I felt that there were even more people than last year bringing their beloved oshi plushies with them to this year’s EXPO. Thanks to all the fans, the plushie photo spot was a big hit and the whole team enjoyed and got so much energy from seeing all of the photos taken and posted to holoplus and on X. We were able to feel the warmth and the excitement from the holoplus community this year and we are so thankful. Please look out for some of the bold new updates we have planned this year to further increase the ways you can enjoy holoplus!⑪hololive Official YouTube Channel Membership (Wallpaper Gallery)The hololive Official YouTube Channel Membership booth was a wallpaper gallery that showed off the vivid colors of the talents. These wallpapers are the same as the ones offered digitally, but enlarged for a clearer view of every stunning detail. The illustrations were minimally edited with color adjustments to bring out the life of the prints, and talents had even graced them with their signatures, making the gallery a must-stop spot for fans looking out for their oshi’s autographs.Q. What was the response like from the fans? Were there any incidents that were unexpected?We set out to make the booth a “relaxing place to chill out” within the arcade, but it was unexpected to see so many fans stop by to take a look. We were glad that the booth was such a success, with all the fans coming to take pictures: their joyous facial expressions left quite an impression on us.We also had feedback from our community questionnaire with people wanting more pictures of the venue, so after discussing it internally, we posted photos of talent standees (panels) and the signature walls from the venue for the members community, which received an overwhelmingly positive response.Q. Was there anything unique to EXPO that you discovered?The hidden messages on the back of standees from other talents written by talents themselves days before the event was such an interesting detail unique to our EXPO! When talents mentioned this on chatting streams before the event, this instantly went to the top of the list of things fans wanted to see, with many fans commenting “I want to see that!” in membership questionnaires. We hope that fans who couldn’t make it to this year’s EXPO or couldn’t get around to seeing everything while they were there enjoyed these photos. ⑫HOUSE OF FLOW GLOW EXPO 2025 ver.HOUSE OF FLOW GLOW EXPO 2025 ver. was a reimagining of FLOW GLOW’s pop-up space in Harajuku held back in November 2024, their debut month. New messages from the members adorned the booth, with fans, decked out in their striking cosplays and long-sleeved tees sold when they debuted, flocking over to show their support for the girls.Q. Tell us about the concept and theme of the booth and anything that left an impression on you.Through all our events held, seeing the number of people who have become fans of our talents and passion they put into enjoying our content gives us the energy and motivation to continue striving to deliver new content. FLOW GLOW will continue to improve their rap skills and deliver even more content that will both surprise and delight fans, and DEV_IS will continue to release a wide range of new and improved music-based content in a variety of forms, so be on the lookout for what’s to come.Q. This was FLOW GLOW’s first EXPO, having debuted in November 2024, but are there features you pay special attention to for someone’s first appearance?We definitely wanted to have FLOW GLOW build up hololive production’s biggest event of the year with the customary EXPO “walking tour,” FLOW GLOW WALKING RADIO – We Tried Overtaking EXPO! -. In addition to the exhibit, we hope that people were able to enjoy the banter and relationship the five members have with each other on the livestream that not only livened up the venue but got more people interested in their individual and unit activities.The Excitement that Interactive Content Brings is What a Festival is All About!At this year’s EXPO, we focused on livening up the ‘fan experience’ for each booth, by having fans play the same games as their oshi and immerse themselves in the lore of each project. The interviews from each booth representative showed how surprised and grateful they were for how lively EXPO was: particularly how moved they were by the fans’ passion, how responses exceeded expectations, and how fans interacted with each other. What was also really noticeable is how active our wonderful fans are in building the community and not just passively viewing our content through conversing with each other while waiting in line and getting creative with merch such as their oshi plushies. Not only that, but the atmosphere of a festival could truly be felt with so many overseas fans in attendance, with interactions not being hindered by language barriers.Check out what attendees had to say about the event in Part 3 of this series.

item.title: CAPSULE x hololive production x “Cheer UP” Event Comes to Los Angeles | NEWS - ホロライブ公式サイト
CAPSULE x hololive production x “Cheer UP” Event Comes to Los Angeles
item.author: Horii
item.content:encoded: COVER Corporation (Minato-ku, Tokyo; CEO: Tanigo Motoaki) has announced that the CAPSULE x hololive production collaboration eventcollaboration event “Cheer UP road running” and “Theme Café” will take place in Los Angeles, USA, this autumn.The collaboration event “Cheer UP” will be held with the cheerleading style of concept, there will be limited merchandise and foods featuring members of hololive production including Oozora Subaru and Usada Pekora of hololive, Kaela Kovalskia and Kobo Kanaeru of hololive Indonesia, and Mori Calliope and Koseki Bijou of hololive English.hololive production Cheer UP Fun Run $125 Ticket Includes: • Runner Bib • Official Event T-shirt • Medal • One Magnetic Badge$185 Ticket Includes Everything Above, Plus: • Sports TowelEarly Bird Perk and Exclusive Merchandise Participants who register by June 15, 2025, will receive a special early registration gift: A randomly selected sticker set.Available only at the run, the official camping chair will be sold as a Fun Run limited item. This special piece is designed as a premium collectible for fans.hololive production Cheer UP Pop-Up CaféDates: October 18 – December 6, 2025 Place: 171 Live Oak Ave Suite D, Arcadia, CA 91006Food and Drinks details will be announced later. #hololiveCheerUP #hololiveFunRun #hololiveCafe #hololiveUSA #OUENEVENTUSA

item.title: hololive SUPER EXPO 2025 Report [Part 1]: A Festival of VTuber Culture Enjoyed With All Five Senses
item.author: cover_yamaguchi
item.content:encoded: An Immersive Experience Like No Other at the hololive ARCADEThis year’s hololive SUPER EXPO (hereinafter ‘EXPO’) was the fourth time the event has been held. It spanned Makuhari Messe International Exhibition Halls 1 to 6, making it the biggest one to date with about 35,000 people in attendance over the two days.The theme for EXPO 2025 was ‘hololive ARCADE.’ It was packed with content, allowing attendees to play, cooperate with, and compete against hololive production members, including at the hololive Amusement Arcade. The EXPO area was split into two: COVER’s booths and the food court located in Halls 1 to 3 and sponsor booths in Halls 4 and 5, which showcased new items, collaboration merchandise, and booths offering a range of experiences.Upon entering the event, attendees were welcomed by an entrance that resembled a claw machine, giving the impression of entering an arcade. Once past the gates, fans were met with a vast arcade center and amusement park-like area, where they could immerse themselves in the EXPO world.In addition to the booths, the EXPO STAGE offered a full day’s worth of fun. Food was packed with the charisma of the talents, and fans could interact with talents during stage shows through a variety of games, creating a lively atmosphere.Treasuring Unity and Memories: A Diversity of Booths Creating a True Arcade ExperienceWhen visiting each unique booth, one could not miss the autographs and messages written by talents and creators. They gave a sense that your oshi was there in person, with queues snaking around the venue and fans eagerly waiting to capture these inscriptions.Each booth had an ‘amusement-like experience with hololive members’ concept, providing experience-based content like exhibits where fans could take photos, games, and attractions where fans could enjoy hololive and HOLOSTARS with all of their senses. We dove a little deeper and got a bit of an insight from some of those who were stationed at these booths.The OFFICIAL GOODS SHOWCASE was an eye-catching exhibit featuring existing and new merchandise in the style of a series of claw machines. The surrounding speakers played specially recorded messages from talents, making it a place for fans to stop and listen while they observed the unique displays.Q. There were various exhibits at the EXPO, but the new merchandise created a lot of buzz on social media. How did you feel when you saw attendees’ reactions online?When we announced on X that there may be information on new merchandise, I was worried that we might not get the kind of excitement we were hoping for, so I was honestly relieved when I saw the number of reactions we received. I didn’t really expect a queue for our booth, so I was astonished! Many people seemed to enjoy the exclusive voice recordings from the talents, so all the preparation we did felt worth it in the end.Q. Please tell us your thoughts about the exhibits and the difficulties you had to overcome.To tell you the truth, claw machines are not suited for merchandise displays. We had to coordinate with many people to put merchandise that was not claw machine prizes into these machines……! However, I was thrilled that many fans were discussing the new merchandise online along with photos of our booth. It felt like we could work with fans to hype up expectations and excitement.Together with the merchandise we showcased, we work hard on developments to keep up with our fanbase, which has grown exponentially over the years. We want to continue using the data we have collected up until now to create merchandise that truly appeals to our fans.②holo Indie & hololive Official GameAt holo Indie & hololive Official Game, hololive-related games were exhibited in two sections. The holo Indie section featured numerous fan-made PC games, where visitors could try out popular titles like Treasure Mountain, which has been played by talents on stream. The Official Title Section featured titles jointly developed with commercial game developers, promotional videos were played, and special edition game merchandise was on display.Q. What kind of reactions did fans have?Although we expected many people to visit our booth and prepared accordingly, I was overwhelmed at how long the lines were for the sticker giveaway and play testing, which made me really happy. I also came across a couple of social media posts where people said they had the most fun at the holo Indie booth. To get such high praise despite there being so many wonderful exhibitions and experiences to be had made all the time and effort we spent on this booth even more worth it.Q. Which game was particularly popular? We’d love to hear what the response from creators was like.The playtesting of games played by talents on stream, such as Treasure Mountain, holo8, and holoRun was especially popular. Many people also tried out yet-to-be-released titles, like MYRIAD DEATH and Chrono Gear: Warden of Time. It was also a fantastic opportunity for people who do not play computer games much to learn more about holo Indie games.Creators have also expressed their gratitude for being given this excellent opportunity for fans of hololive to try out their games. holo Indie is supported by the innovative work of these creators, so I hope that we can generate more interest in our games among attendees through events like these.The booth for the media-mix project HOLOLIVE ALTERNATIVE was an immersive space featuring a series of stunning pieces of artwork. One of the main attractions was the live drawing exhibition from the manga artists* for Yamato Phantasia, Underworld Academy Overload!!, and Vesta de Cooking. Many fans witnessed some amazing illustrations being brought to life in front of their eyes!Q. Please tell us more about the booth’s concept and themes, and any incidents or plans that left an impression on you.We wanted to create a fresh way for people to learn about HOLOLIVE ALTERNATIVE, so we built a booth with an immersive atmosphere. The live drawings from the manga artists were perfected over time, making it something that people could enjoy no matter how often they returned to check on the progress, so I would consider that a success.“Even though it was all a first for me, I think I was able to draw a great piece of artwork without any major slip-ups, thanks to the passion of the holoListeners (viewers of hololive) pulling me through. I had so much fun and am thankful for the opportunity to attend EXPO and be a part of such a wonderful experience,” said Kurose Kousuke, author of Yamato Phantasia.Q. Please tell us more about any future developments.In the manga, Underworld Academy Overload!!, a new chapter has started with the appearance of Hakoz Baelz, and Volume 3 of Yamato Phantasia and Volume 1 of Vesta de Cooking were released on May 7th! Additionally, the 2D pixel art action game FUBUKI 〜zero in on Holoearth〜 HOLOLIVE ALTERNATIVE is now out on Steam, and we have even more content coming soon, so we hope everyone is looking forward to what’s to come.④hololive Amusement ArcadeThe hololive Amusement Arcade was a collection of game booths designed to suit the theme of EXPO, where fans could play actual games for the first time at EXPO, posing a new challenge for those in booth development. Fans could challenge themselves to try and get a higher score than what the talents recorded, in games like Hammer Free Fall and holoWars.Q. Please give us an insight into the effort that went into making these games, and what difficulties you encountered.We paid extra attention to making the talents’ presence as realistic as possible to provide an experience where players were essentially “playing with the talents.” This became the booth at this EXPO that had the most input from talents because we had to meticulously create videos and voice recordings for it. Talents also put their ideas into the games and had fun testing them before the event.The most challenging part was the safety measures. As games required the swinging of hammers and shooting balls in the pachinko game, we had to consider the safety of those playing and the people around them. We prepared hammers of three different weights, placed safety nets around the booths, and stationed staff all around the booths to ensure proper surveillance. I was really relieved that everyone had fun without any accidents.Q. Was there anything that left an impression on you?As the day wore on, people waiting in line started to cheer on those playing the games and applaud them when they did well. Those heartwarming interactions among fans left a rather deep impression on me. It was truly a sight to see how battered the pedestals used for the games became from everyone putting in their all (lol). I believe that fans were able to create some really fond memories and have so much fun that they left their mark on the pedestals.The concept of HOLOSTARS Battle Street was based on watching a fight in a back alley, with a street art-style design inspired by fighting games. Shows started as early as 10 AM, with each show being broadcast live and featuring the talents of HOLOSTARS singing and gaming. Enthusiastic fans were spotted grabbing the best spots in the middle at the front of the viewing area just 10 minutes after doors opened.Q. Please tell us about any incidents or fans that left an impression on you.It would be hard to go past the fans who came to our booth first thing in the morning, eagerly anticipating the shows, some with their handmade happis to support their oshis. Cosplayers visited the booth in all versions of their favorite talents, coming in not just their regular outfits, but exclusive outfits that only featured in seasonal voice packs and events, the latest Starry outfit, and even EXPO2025 outfits. There were even cosplayers with outfits from voice packs that were only announced just weeks prior!It was also amazing to see the interaction between overseas and Japanese HOLOSTARS fans, who were exchanging illustrations and confectionery.Q. Please tell us what you had to pay the most attention to for this EXPO.We made sure that the talents appearing throughout the program did something game battle-related to suit the name of the booth! They not only hosted games among themselves but also got the help of fans using the camera to complete a game together for instance, so that viewers could truly play a part in the games. We put extra effort into making content that made it easy for people who did not know HOLOSTARS to remember the traits of each member.⑥Magical Girl holoWitches!At the Magical Girl holoWitches! booth, a range of mirror maze-style exhibits, and voice recordings from talents made for an extra magical experience. Life-sized standees of talents and photo areas that brought backgrounds from the 3D animated performance “Magical Girl holoWitches! THE STAGE” to life glamorized the booth and the special sticker booths, where one could take photos with their favorite talents, were a highlight among fans.Q. Please tell us about any incidents or visitors that left an impression on you.Many fans came decked out in merchandise and cosplay based on the series, their passion clearly on show, while others carried bags decorated neatly with the badges (called itabags) of their oshi holoWitches, which they lined up for taking photos for social media. There were also those who took the opportunity to get holoWitches cosplayers together: the booth serving as a positive space for the community to interact, which was so rewarding to see.Q. Why did you want to use printed stickers for the booth? Please share any stories related to this project if you can.We had the idea of printed stickers as we thought that they could become a piece of memorabilia that visitors could take home from the experience. In the festival-like atmosphere of the EXPO, there were so many fans who put so much effort into their outfits and brought along their own merchandise, so it was the perfect souvenir for fans to take home as a reminder of their wonderful time spent at the photo booth.We put a lot of effort into designing the holoWitches-themed printed stickers, and used scripts that were recorded specially by the talents for the photo booth. Everything was carefully configured and designed just for EXPO2025.

item.title: Cover Corp Announces Record Revenue for FY2025
item.author: Thomas Toblu
item.content:encoded: Cover Corp is the company behind the global VTuber sensation hololive. The company has just released its financial report for FY2025/3 (April 2024–March 2025). It was a record-breaking year. There was massive growth across all sectors of the business. This includes concerts and streaming. It also covers trading cards and global licensing deals.If you’re a VTuber fan, a business enthusiast, or just curious about the industry’s booming numbers, this article breaks it all down for you.Cover’s Last Quarterly ReportAnycolor’s Latest Quarterly ReportKey Takeaways at a GlanceA Year of Explosive GrowthCover Corp reported total revenue of , a whopping over the previous year. This growth wasn’t isolated to one area. Instead, it was spread across all major revenue streams:This performance blew past Cover’s revised earnings forecasts, driven by merchandise, global concerts, and licensing expansion.Merchandising: The Star PlayerMerchandise, especially the hololive Official Card Game (TCG), was a key driver. The TCG was launched in . It exceeded expectations in sales and community engagement. Over participated in related events like store tournaments and fan meetups.Beyond cards, Cover ramped up retail distribution. They diversified their product offerings to include everything from apparel and plushies to made-to-order VTuber anniversary goods.TCG revenue was the highest ever for a single product line.Total shipments hit (up +48% YoY).Q4 merchandise sales alone reached ¥6.28 billion (+42.3% YoY).Concerts and Events: Going Globalhololive has fully embraced its status as a global entertainment brand. This year saw the and a second North American live concert for hololive English.15 concerts held globally (up from 10 last year)520,000 visitors total (+53% YoY)hololive SUPER EXPO and 6th Fes hit with 260,000 fansThe blend of digital stardom with real-world engagement helped boost fan loyalty and revenue. Large-scale concerts at prestigious venues like Nippon Budokan and Ariake Arena elevated the brand to new heights.Streaming and Content: Still the CoreStreaming remains the backbone of Cover’s IP-building efforts. Revenue here grew by as both new and legacy talents expanded their reach.2,792 average monthly unique users (+36.7%)118,953 total videos produced (+29.6%)Music exposure skyrocketed—VTubers like sang anime theme songs, including one for Mobile Suit Gundam GQuuuuuuX., , and ranked among the top five most-watched female streamers worldwide, even outside the VTuber space.Licensing and Collaborations: Expanding Beyond Otaku CultureCover grew its licensing portfolio significantly, closing deals with major players across diverse industries:Sony PlayStation (30th anniversary ad)Their new North American business base began operation mid-year and helped onboard more overseas partners. The number of corporate partners rose from .Holoearth and Digital ExpansionThe long-awaited metaverse platform hit a major milestone in April 2025. This was marked by the launch of the . It lets fans create and trade fashion items and stamps.Avatar tracking via webcam in developmentUpcoming virtual concerts and meet-and-greets with VTubersHoloearth development started showing on the books this year¥500M in amortization costs (expected to double in FY2026/3)Cover’s profit margins stayed healthy despite increased expenses from events and R&D:The company maintains strong cash flow, providing flexibility for investments and future acquisitions.Looking Ahead: FY2026/3 and BeyondCover expects continued growth in FY2026/3, with projected revenue of . However, it anticipates due to:Increased upfront investments in overseas expansionR&D for new content delivery systems and streaming technologiesDevelopment costs for Holoearth and game projects: Operating profit forecast is only slightly higher at ¥8.2B (+2.5%).Cover isn’t just growing—it’s aiming to become a core player in the global content market.Estimated market size: ¥105BGlobal content market: ¥123.6TCover’s plan is to grow VTubers into a transmedia IP force rivaling anime, games, and traditional influencers.Key Expansion Strategies:Localized merchandise and logistics in NA and AsiaJoint anime/game projectsGlobal TCG expansion (English version coming)More strategic alliances and potential M&A activityThe People Behind the VisionUnder the leadership of and a seasoned management team, Cover has been scaling rapidly. Recent structural changes introduced new Senior Vice Presidents overseeing merchandising, overseas development, VTuber production, and metaverse initiatives.With nearly , the company is growing fast—and smart.Anti-Defamation and ESG MeasuresCover took action on over the past year to protect its talents. It also joined major industry associations like:Japan Online Game AssociationMusic Publishers AssociationSafer Internet AssociationTheir anti-defamation study group (in collaboration with Google and other VTuber agencies) continues to push for healthier online ecosystems.Final Thoughts: A New Era for Virtual EntertainmentCover Corp’s FY2025/3 report paints a picture of a company that’s not only expanding rapidly but also itself.Investing in creator tools and digital platformsStrengthening their global footprintBuilding a diversified, multi-billion-yen businessWith the success of hololive and their expanding content empire, Cover is poised to achieve great things. They are ready to redefine what it means to be an entertainment company in the 21st century.Whether you’re a fan, investor, or business observer, one thing is clear. The VTuber revolution is just getting started. Cover is at the front of the parade.

item.title: hololive production Collaboration with Manhattan Portage | NEWS - ホロライブ公式サイト
hololive production Collaboration with Manhattan Portage - カバー株式会社
hololive production Collaboration with Manhattan Portage
item.author: Horii
item.content:encoded: COVER Corporation announced a collaboration with the iconic New York bag brand, Manhattan Portage. This collaboration will feature hololive English talents from hololive English -Advent- and hololive English -Justice-, in a special collection that merges the unique styles of these VTubers with Manhattan Portage’s urban aesthetic. Manhattan Portage, celebrated for its “New York Tough” ethos and high-quality bags designed for the dynamic urban environment, has been a signature brand all across the globe. This partnership aims to bring together the vibrant energy of hololive English -Advent- and hololive English -Justice- with Manhattan Portage’s commitment to creating practical, stylish, and enduring products! The collaboration will feature a range of bags, starting from Manhattan Portage’s signature messenger bag to waist bags, backpacks, and mini shoulder bags, designed with special themes inspired by hololive English -Advent- and hololive English -Justice-, incorporating their distinctive colors and logo designs.hololive production Collaboration with Manhattan PortagePre-Order Period: May 8th, 2025 11:00AM – June 8th, 2025 (EST) Born in New York City in 1983, Manhattan Portage designs durable and functional bags for urban living. The brand’s iconic messenger bags and other products are known for their quality craftsmanship and timeless style, embodying the “New York Tough” spirit.

The Future of VTubers: Shigeaki Hayashi SVP on the Creative Production Department’s Pursuit of Innovation
item.author: cover_hirata
item.content:encoded: First, the Illustration Production Division is made up of around 20 young staff members who are responsible for designing costumes and creating key visuals. In their personal time, many of them are actively engaged in creative activities, such as sharing their artwork on social media and participating in UGC culture.The Computer Graphic Production Division consists of 3D character and background designers and technical artists, along with creators with strong craftsmanship skills. Many of the team members have experience in game development, and the team features a wide range of experience levels from young professionals to veterans.The Production Promotion Division is responsible for project management and livestream operations. The team is made up of diverse members from various backgrounds – including former YouTubers and global talent – who work together to drive the project forward. The Livestream Technology Division is responsible for technical supervision, filming, sound, application management, and motion capture. The division is made up of many members who approach studio maintenance, equipment management, and recording with the utmost dedication and care. The Livestream Planning Division handles production planning, the creation of variety shows and music live events, and the production of streaming assets. The team includes many specialists in online streaming, particularly on platforms like YouTube, and is made up of youthful, energetic members who are actively driving projects forward.The Technology Development Division is made up of developers, quality control specialists, and art engineers. While many of the members come from the gaming industry, the team also includes members with extensive knowledge in AR and VR technologies. Their work covers a wide range of areas, including developing and maintaining apps for talents and studio operations, enhancing the functionality of studio apps, and creating special effects and features used in livestreams.

item.title: OMOCAT x hololive English -Myth- New Plushie Collection - ホロライブ公式サイト
OMOCAT x hololive English -Myth- New Plushie Collection
item.author: Horii
item.content:encoded: COVER Corporation has announced that the pre-order of OMOCAT x hololive English -Myth- plushie collection started on April 30th at 5:00 PM (PST).OMOCAT x hololive English -Myth- plushie collection, a collaboration project between hololive English -Myth- and OMOCAT, an apparel brand known for its popularity in the U.S. and worldwide, released its new collection, featuring the talents of hololive English -Myth-. This collection includes 6.5″ tall plushies made with 100% polyester/100% PP cotton.Pre-order is available on the OMOCAT website. Please check it out!OMOCAT x hololive English -Myth- plushie collection Line-up: Takanashi Kiara plush Gawr Gura plush* Will be Shipped from the United States, and the expected arrival for the items will be around October 2025. For more information on shipping, please visit the OMOCAT official website. OMOCAT is a L.A.-based indie apparel and merchandise brand that eclectically blends together illustration, anime subculture, and street fashion. Established in 2013, OMOCAT’s original take on graphic apparel has garnered the attention of art enthusiasts and the pop culture community through the years.
